Message: Damp air rises from the stair behind you. Somewhere below these halls, the one you came for might still be alive.
The city noise fades quickly above. The Wake begins in silence and wet stone.
The first corridors feel abandoned. You picture them standing here, deciding whether to go deeper.
Water drips through cracked stone. If they came this way, their footsteps once echoed here too.
The descent begins gently enough. You tell yourself they could still be close.
The tunnels narrow and twist. It is easy to imagine someone losing their way down here.
Old tool marks scar the walls. Someone built these halls long ago.
The deeper air smells colder. You wonder how long they have been gone.
You pause at each intersection, listening for a voice you know will not answer.
Every corridor feels like a choice they once had to make.
Water runs down the walls in thin streams. The sound almost resembles whispering.
Broken tools lie scattered in the dust. Whoever worked here never finished their task.
The deeper tunnels bend in deliberate patterns. This was design, not accident.
You imagine them pushing forward into the depths, just as you are now.
The Wake begins to feel less like ruins and more like a path.
The stonework grows smoother here. These passages were shaped with careful intention.
Strange carvings appear in the walls. Warnings, perhaps, though you cannot read them.
The deeper corridors loop strangely, as if the dungeon resists being mapped.
You imagine them realizing the same thing: this place was built for a purpose.
The air feels heavy with age. Someone long ago decided this descent mattered.
The stone here is older than anything above. You can feel it; the Wake truly begins below.
The walls feel strangely warm, as if something deep beneath them still lives.
The silence grows deeper here. Even the dripping water fades away.
If they reached this far, they must have realized the tunnels were only the beginning.
The path ahead feels deliberate now, like a descent into the heart of something ancient.
Bones line the walls in careful rows. You try not to search them for a familiar face.
Dust rises around your steps. These dead have watched many Delvers pass through.
Crypts stretch into the darkness. You wonder if someone you love once walked here alone.
The air is dry and still. Whoever built these halls thought the dead to stay quiet.
The deeper you go, the more the Wake begins to resemble a grave.
Names cover the walls, carved into ancient stone. You read every one.
On the wall, a freshly carved inscriptions. Someone else came searching, too.
Bones are stacked carelessly here, as if something disturbed their rest.
A cracked marker bears a half-erased name. You feel a cold stab of panic before realizing it isn't their name.
The catacombs feel less like a resting place; more and more, this is a record.
The bones are scattered here. Something has passed through, long after the burials ended.
Messages scratched into the walls beg for rescue that never came.
The silence feels wrong; the dead themselves are listening.
You imagine them moving through these crypts with the same uneasy hope.
Every empty alcove makes you wonder who never left.
Strange patterns repeat in the carvings. Names appear twice, sometimes three times.
You see their name etched in the stone in a glance; you look back, but it is no longer a name you recognize.
The deeper catacombs feel unsettled, as if memory itself is shifting.
The walls hold more names than there were bodies to bury.
Something about the Wake refuses to let the past remain still; the dead do not rest easy here.
As you enter, you step over bones were arranged deliberately as a barrier.
A cracked stone seal lies broken in the floor. Something beneath it was meant to stay buried.
This chamber feels ominous. Your instincts scream of dire risk ahea.
The catacombs must be drawing to a close. How much deeper is the Wake?
Did they know what the Wake was hiding, when they made it through the catacombs?
Stone walls carry metal here. Pipes and gears twist through the rock like veins.
The smell of oil replaces dust and bone. There are machines beneath the dead.
Rusted mechanisms turn slowly overhead. The Wake is still running.
This is not ruins, not a crypt- more like an engine.
You wonder what they thought when they first saw the iron mechanisms adorning the walls this deep in the Wake.
The machines grow in fervent motion, filling entire chambers with sounds of grinding metal.
Pistons rise and fall in slow rhythm. The Wake breathes like a buried engine.
Hot metal glows faintly in the dark. No one remains to care for the machines.
The deeper you go, the clearer it becomes: this place was meant to function forever.
If they came this far, they must have realized the dungeon was never abandoned.
Heat travels through the walls in humming pipes. Power still flows beneath the earth.
The floor vibrates steadily under your boots.
Strange markings label the machinery, written in symbols older than the city above.
The Wake feels alive here, quietly stirring in the shadows.
You wonder what became of the missing Delvers who came before you.
Corridors converge toward a single shaft descending into darkness.
The machinery grows louder, the sound echoing through metal chambers.
Everything above seems designed to guide you toward what lies below.
The deeper mechanisms of the Wake reveal themselves, pulsing, beckoning your forward.
If they reached this depth, they knew the descent was no accident.
All the machinery leads here, circling something far older than the dungeon itself.
The air is heavy with memory; countless minds have stood where you stand.
You sense a presence in the chamber, both familiar and distant, and it is waiting for you.
The Wake was built to hold something, but it feels incomplete.
For the first time since entering the Wake, you believe yourself close to finding them.
